decision.recommended_next_action: Finalize this validation; treat format-control and larger-model scaling as a separate follow-on study rather than continuing the same project.
decision.stop_reason: The stronger Qwen2.5-1.5B live RAG benchmark showed a 36.31-point answer-quality gain at the same budget while using 6.80% fewer prompt tokens, satisfying the success criterion with live-model evidence.

recommended_next_action: Finalize this validation; treat format-control and larger-model scaling as a separate follow-on study rather than continuing the same project.
run_notes_tail: ## Execution plan (2026-04-18, stronger local LLM validation)
1. Reuse the existing OpenAI-compatible benchmark harness without changing the packer or scorer, so the new run is directly comparable to the earlier Qwen2.5-0.5B result.
2. Start a stronger cached local model from a neutral services directory, verify `/v1/models` and `/v1/chat/completions`, and run a short latency/resource smoke before the benchmark.
3. Execute the real-RAG benchmark on the same 14-task SQuAD/Wikipedia LLM slice, recording answer quality, prompt tokens, latency, throughput, UMA memory, server RSS/PSS, and GPU utilization.
4. Stop the helper server, update durable result artifacts, and decide whether the original hypothesis has enough live-model support to finalize.
## Implementation/results (2026-04-18 stronger local LLM validation)

- Launched cached `Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct` with vLLM from `/tmp/omx_llm_services/answer_shape_planner_qwen15b` on `http://127.0.0.1:18001/v1` using `--max-model-len 2048` and `--gpu-memory-utilization 0.35`.
- Verified `/v1/models` returned `qwen2.5-1.5b-instruct` and a JSON-only `/v1/chat/completions` smoke returned valid `{"ok": true}` in about `0.155 s`.
- Ran `experiments/run_real_rag_benchmark.py` through the OpenAI-compatible hook on the same broader SQuAD/Wikipedia slice and wrote `results/real_rag_benchmark_llm_qwen15b.json` plus `.md`.
- Stopped the vLLM helper server before yielding so no project-directory server remains running.
## Verification (2026-04-18 stronger local LLM validation)

- `python3 experiments/run_real_rag_benchmark.py --out results/real_rag_benchmark_llm_qwen15b.json --max-tasks 16 --llm-base-url http://127.0.0.1:18001/v1 --llm-model qwen2.5-1.5b-instruct --server-pid 607797` completed.
- `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -v` passed 4 tests.
- `python3 -m py_compile src/answer_shape_prompt_planner.py experiments/run_real_rag_benchmark.py` passed.
## Key metrics (local LLM endpoint, Qwen2.5-1.5B)
- Dataset/tasks: SQuAD dev v1.1 Wikipedia contexts, 14 tasks.
- Answer quality: planner `0.3690` vs uniform `0.0060` (`+36.31` points).
- Prompt tokens: planner `120.50` vs uniform `129.29` (`6.80%` fewer).
- Field F1: planner `0.5476` vs uniform `0.0119`.
- JSON exact match: planner `0.0000` vs uniform `0.0000`.
- Citation support: planner `0.2143` vs uniform `0.0714`.
- Total latency: planner `780.70 ms` vs uniform `725.96 ms`; answer latency p50/p95 `658.66/1962.24 ms`.
- Completion throughput: `55.75` tokens/sec; sampled GPU utilization `95%`; server RSS/PSS `3800/3457 MB`; MemAvailable stayed about `73.6 GB` and swap free about `130.0 GB`.
## Interpretation and stop decision (2026-04-18 stronger local LLM validation)

The stronger live-model run resolves the main uncertainty from the 0.5B pilot: answer-shape packing produces a large task-native quality gain at the same budget while still using fewer prompt tokens. JSON exact match and citation formatting remain weak, but the planner improves field recovery and citation support rather than merely exploiting the deterministic scorer. Combined with the deterministic synthetic, answer-quality, and real-RAG results, the original hypothesis is now supported strongly enough to finalize this project positively. Further work should be a separate format-control/scaling study, not another generic continuation of the same validation loop.
## Execution plan (2026-04-18, stronger local LLM validation)
1. Reuse the existing OpenAI-compatible benchmark harness without changing the packer or scorer, so the new run is directly comparable to the earlier Qwen2.5-0.5B result.
2. Start a stronger cached local model from a neutral services directory, verify `/v1/models` and `/v1/chat/completions`, and run a short latency/resource smoke before the benchmark.
3. Execute the real-RAG benchmark on the same 14-task SQuAD/Wikipedia LLM slice, recording answer quality, prompt tokens, latency, throughput, UMA memory, server RSS/PSS, and GPU utilization.
4. Stop the helper server, update durable result artifacts, and decide whether the original hypothesis has enough live-model support to finalize.

## Key metrics (local LLM endpoint, Qwen2.5-1.5B)
